instructions: Claustodian is a provenance-strict dataset: its data must come ONLY from official, first-party, public sources (the Claude Code changelog, the official Claude Code documentation pages at code.claude.com/docs, and officially published release binaries), never leaked, source-map-derived, or otherwise non-public material. Guard that invariant. Question every "temporary" workaround that has outlived its excuse.

rules.rule: Data may originate ONLY from official, first-party, public Anthropic sources: the official CHANGELOG.md, the official Claude Code documentation pages (code.claude.com/docs), and officially published release binaries (GitHub release assets, npm-published bundles). These map to the `provenance` values `changelog`, `docs`, and `binary` respectively. Flag any code path that could ingest leaked, source-map-derived, or otherwise non-public material, or that blurs which lane a record came from.

JSON under data/ is the generated source of truth, produced by `npm run scrape`, and YAML/TOML are build artifacts. Changes to symbol data belong in the scraper/schema, not in hand-edited data files. Don't suggest editing generated output directly.

CLI entrypoints are tested in-process by calling the exported main() with process.argv set and temp dirs (see the *.cli.test.ts files) so v8 coverage counts them. Prefer that pattern over spawning subprocesses, which get zero coverage credit and can hit the network.
